I don't know how much you go in.

I mean, we're familiar with the space, right?
With some of your competitors, products, et cetera.

Well, so eager to dive in
for sure.
And, and I always say it's, it's a pretty easy conversation.
So I think there's four things that make this product really unique.
Um The first thing being is that we're fully automated, so there's zero manual call around kind of inputting of data whatsoever.
Uh whatsoever, either from our team or from your team.
Um We also update our data every single day.
So as I'm sure you're aware, you know, properties out there, they're on these rev tools, they're changing rates regularly.
So our goal is that anytime you want to understand your market, you can jump into our platform and see what's going on today.

Um The third thing that's nice is we can track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software, they do not have to be, our customers are participating in our program whatsoever.
So we can truly track the entire market.
And um more specifically, I'll let you know.
So our, our, our tool goes up to these property websites every single night and then we get the unit level data from the online leasing section of these property websites.
So that's why I was saying in order for us to track a uh a comp they have to have a website and use the property management software.
Um The last thing that I like to mention is that everything inside of apartment IQ is 100% publicly available data.
And the reason that I bring that up now was just in terms or I guess in light of the the real page lawsuit.
Now the yard lawsuit kind of, you know, you know, the antitrust sharing of private data, we have nothing to do with that whatsoever.
So if that is a concern, just know that this is, there's zero private information here.
Um, over the last six months here, I've dealt with more of the legal side of this than I ever thought I would have to.
But nonetheless, we, uh, we've been put through the wringer with some of these more litigious companies always come out very clean.
So 100% publicly available data.
So, um, but yeah, let me walk you through it.
I um definitely ask questions along the way
you see that.

So typically, the way this works is whenever we set these up is that you would give us a list of the comps that your team's been tracking and then we'd start there.
However, we do have a comp matching algorithm where basically we look around at every property in the vicinity of your property.
And we let you know how well it comps out so we can provide some additional insights of who we think you should uh track.
I think it's particularly helpful in the markets like Phoenix where there's a lot of new inventory coming on board or perhaps a lot of renovations happening.

So again, just making sure you're tracking everybody who is relevant, but nonetheless, you're in control of your comp set and then directly inside the app itself as well, you always have the ability to add or remove properties anytime you'd like.
So,
um but yeah, like I said, each property gets its own card and then to view the analytics for a property, you click the view button and it'll take you inside the data.

So each property is four reports associated with it.
The first report which run here is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, there's gonna be three additional reports as well.
So I'll go over each one very high level for you.
I won't drag you through the weeds.

So you can look at the data based on a specific comp or even a subset of comps.
You can also look at it based off of bedrooms, bathrooms, square footage range, rent, price range, et cetera.
Then down below here, all of these metrics are sort from low to high and high to low.

Uh We simply list all of your competitors across the top and then we also include your property which would be highlighted here in the Indigo.
And then I think we did a really nice job of making this as easy as possible to compare your property's performance versus the competition.
Um There are three different sections here.
The first one being property summary, that's a lot of your high level property data, some of this performance based metrics.
So we'll show you things like year built, total, number of units advertised occupancy and I'll come back to this in a second.
Uh We show you exposure percentage over a couple of different time horizons.
We'll also let you know the number of units that are available inside of each property of those that are available.
How many are vacant?
We'll also show you some transactional data.
So the number of leases that each property had over the last 30 days, seven days and the number of leases that each property does average per week.
And then last of the big one is we will let you know if there's a rent special or concession tied to the property or tied to a specific floor plan or a unit in the property.

And we do take this a step further and we'll actually calculate net effective rent and net effective price per foot for you at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level.
Any questions ever before I explain, advertised the occupancy and how we track things like leases.
No, I, I don't go ahead.

So the big one that I like to call it is advertised occupancy.
So the reason we call this advertised occupancy, if instead of just true occupancy is because it's based off of the known number of vacant units in the property.
And this actually has to do with the public versus private data process.
So properties have different processes in terms of how they advertise units as, as available on the website.
And what this really means is, and it's more typical in a lease up.
But it's a, if a property has, let's say they have 31 bedrooms available, they decide to only make 10 of them available on the property website.
Our system can only account for the 10 that are made available on the property website.
We have no clue about those other 20 that are hidden.
And I always say, I mean, from a, I guess a sales perspective here, like neither does a render they visit the website, that's what they see, that's what they're going off of.
Um And then like the hidden inventory, like there's 20 hidden units.
I mean that extra supply is still being influenced in the price.
It is being shown on the, the 10 that are available.
So that's advertised occupancy
um in terms of leases.
So in terms of the transactional data, so since we're tracking each unit individually, if that unit is available on the website today, and not tomorrow, we assume that that unit has been leased
on a different report will actually report the least price.
And the way that we do that is based off of the last lowest advertised price in the property website.
So that's the process there.

Um What I will say that's really nice about apartment IQ compared to a call around and compared to RADI is if you ever have a question where uh a data point, a metric of calculation is coming from, I can pull up the source and show you exactly where we're getting it from as opposed to kind of what a leasing agent told you.
Is it made up?
Is it the truth?
Is it what's available?

So I I've got a good stat for you.
So our, our database right now, we're close to 17 million units that we track nightly over 60,000 properties hiding um availability is, is very common in a lease up.
But for our entire database, for all of those stabilized communities, all those stabilized properties, we found that 6.6% of properties hide availability.
So it does happen for sure.
Um But yeah, it's, it's 6.6% which I'm not saying is insignificant, but it's not 50% or, or 80% or anything like that.

Is there any, is there any notifier or asterisk anywhere on here that will like indicate for a competitor, whether they are a comp that shows their full contingent of available units or whether they're a comp that will hide and play the supply demand game?
Yeah.
So what's interesting is we actually can, our tool is smart enough, smart enough to see full availability.
We've actually developed how to do it.

So the reason we don't do that is technically private data.
So we're in the process right now of actually figuring out a way to indicate that a property is hiding availability but still fall in line with, with public information.
So to answer your question, we do have that capability.
It's just not pre presented in the, in the, in the product right now.

But if they're offering a concession, we could probably make it an assumption that they're, you know, I don't know if you need to necessarily offer a concession or offer a month free rent if you only have 41 bedrooms available type.
So kind of putting the pieces together.
That way, I think you can make assumptions that they are, that they are hiding units.
But what's interesting?

Right?

But what is the incidence rate of errors for supply data being accurate versus not accurate?
So our, our error rate is less than 1%.

There's, there's
no way to prevent that.
